Code fragment: public final TableField<OtCourseRecord, Long> ID_TEACHER = createField(DSL.name("ID_TEACHER"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The ID_TEACHER column appears to reference the OtUser table (teachers are users) but lacks a foreign key constraint, which could lead to orphaned records or invalid teacher references. Add a foreign key constraint referencing the OtUser.ID_USER primary key to ensure referential integrity.

Code fragment: public final TableField<OtCourseRecord, Double> PRICE = createField(DSL.name("PRICE"), SQLDataType.FLOAT.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The PRICE column uses FLOAT type which can cause rounding errors when storing monetary values, potentially leading to incorrect calculations. Change the column type to DECIMAL or NUMERIC with appropriate precision and scale (e.g., DECIMAL(10,2)) to ensure accurate financial calculations.

Code fragment: public final TableField<OtRoleMenuRecord, Long> ID_ROLE_MENU = createField(DSL.name("ID_ROLE_MENU"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: This table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (ID_ROLE_MENU) even though it has unique constraints on ID_ROLE and ID_MENU that could serve as a composite natural primary key. Since this is a junction table linking roles to menus, consider using a composite primary key of (ID_ROLE, ID_MENU) instead of the synthetic ID_ROLE_MENU column.

Code fragment: public final TableField<OtUserCourseRecord, Long> ID_USER_COURSE = createField(DSL.name("ID_USER_COURSE"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");

Consider using a composite primary key on (ID_USER, ID_COURSE) instead of the synthetic ID_USER_COURSE column.
